t_ch: 签下「{NICKNAME}」
t_en: (Sign as "{NICKNAME}")

t_ch: 下一位。
t_en: Next!

t_ch: 签下「模糊二维马」
t_en: (Sign as "Blurry_QR_Colt")

t_ch: 签下「{NICKNAME}」
t_en: (Sign as "{NICKNAME}")

t_ch: 签下「三月七」
t_en: (Sign as "March 7th")

t_ch: 下一位。
t_en: Next!

t_ch: 人生中最漫长的那天结束时，你的手几乎失去了知觉。好在那卷透明胶带确实还算管用。
t_en: By the time the longest day of your life finally ends, your hand is completely numb. At least that roll of tape actually worked.

t_ch: 我真的需要一台机铠！
t_en: I really need a mechatron!

t_ch: 不好意思，小朋友，你真的来错地方了。
t_en: I'm sorry, kiddo, but you've really come to the wrong place.

t_ch: 难道…这里不是珠星大厦吗？
t_en: Wait... isn't this Pearluxe Tower?

t_ch: 是，但这边是研发区，不是销售区。想要机铠，去前台结账就能带回家了。
t_en: It is, but this is the R&D wing, not the shop. If you want a mechatron, you can buy one at the front desk.

t_ch: 我不要量产机，很容易死于不明AOE。要继承破晓战队的意志，得有更强的力量才行……
t_en: I don't want a mass-produced model. They just get wiped out by random crossfire. If I'm going to carry on the legacy of the Daybreak Squadron, I need something way stronger...

t_ch: 你们肯定有的吧，那种还在实验中、没有对外公开的超~强型号！记得叫…「主角机」。请给我「主角机」，求求了！
t_en: You've got to have one, right? One of those suuuper-strong experimental models that hasn't been released yet! I think it's called... a "Protagonist." Please, can I have a "Protagonist"? Pretty please!

t_ch: 这…不太好办……
t_en: That's... not exactly how things work...

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 抱歉打扰您了，参观区域仍正常开放……
t_en: Pardon the interruption, but the visitor area is still open as usual...

t_ch: 天呐，看站姿就知道你强得可怕，一定是机铠领域高手！能帮帮我吗？我真的需要一台机铠。
t_en: Whoa! I can tell just by the way you're standing that you're super strong! You must be a mechatron pro. Can you help me? I really, really need a mechatron.

t_ch: 主角机？在我手上呢。
t_en: Protagonist? I have it.

t_ch: 天呐！看站姿就知道你是主角，难怪有「主角机」。能不能借我用用？求你了！
t_en: Woah! I knew you were the main character just by looking at you! That explains why you have a Protagonist. Can I borrow it? Please!

t_ch: （这孩子…难道从没来过玩具店吗？）
t_en: (Poor kid... has he seriously never been to a toy store?)

t_ch: 小朋友，我们到旁边去聊吧，站在这里会影响客人的。
t_en: Hey kid, let's go chat over there. We're blocking the other visitors.

t_ch: 唔，好吧。但不许搪塞我！
t_en: Okay, fine. But you'd better not be lying to me!

t_ch: 拜托了，我真的很需要一台机铠。这是我一生的请求！
t_en: Please, I'm begging you! I really need a mechatron. This is the most important thing in my entire life!

t_ch: 你为什么需要机铠？
t_en: Why do you need a mechatron so badly?

t_ch: 因为破晓战队就是用机铠作战的，我也要像他们一样，驾驶机铠去战斗。
t_en: Because the Daybreak Squadron uses them to fight, and I want to be just like them. I want to pilot one, too!

t_ch: 小朋友还是赶快回家吧。
t_en: You should really head on home, kid.

t_ch: 我不是小孩，我已经<unbreak>10</unbreak>岁了。更何况我也没有家…现在的我只想驾驶机铠去战斗！
t_en: I'm not a kid! I'm ten years old! Besides... I don't even have a home. All I want to do is pilot a mechatron and fight!

t_ch: 我听说，破晓战队被击败了。可那是破晓战队，是无论何时都会团结一致、向坏人挥拳的英雄啊。英雄怎么会输呢……
t_en: I heard the Daybreak Squadron got beat. But that can't be right! They're heroes! They always stick together and punch the bad guys. Heroes aren't supposed to lose...

t_ch: 我不相信他们会输。当初灰烬来医院探望我的时候说过，英雄任何时候都不会认输。
t_en: I don't believe it. When Cinderborne came to see me in the hospital, he told me that a real hero never, ever gives up.

t_ch: 所以，我要继承他的意志，向大家证明破晓战队还没有输！他们帮助过我，现在，我也想帮助他们。
t_en: So I'm going to carry on his legacy and show everyone that the Daybreak Squadron is still in the fight! They helped me when I was down, and now I want to help them back!

t_ch: 但没有机铠的话，谁会相信我能代表破晓战队呢……
t_en: But without a mechatron, nobody's going to believe that I can represent the Daybreak Squadron...

t_ch: 你说灰烬去探望过你？
t_en: You said Cinderborne came to visit you once?

t_ch: 是啊，破晓战队经常去医院探望生病的孩子们，还有电视台记者跟踪报道呢。
t_en: Yeah, Daybreak Squadron visits sick kids at the hospital all the time. There's even TV crews covering it.

t_ch: 多亏灰烬的鼓励，现在我痊愈了。果然，真正的英雄连病毒都能击败！
t_en: Thanks to Cinderborne's encouragement, I'm better now. No wonder they say no virus can defeat a true hero!

t_ch: 只要有机铠就行了吧？
t_en: All you need is a mechatron, isn't it?

t_ch: 需要机铠的话，我手头就有一台——最大最酷的那个就是。
t_en: I happen to have a mechatron at hand. The biggest, baddest one right there.

t_ch: 稍等，我核查下…没错，那台机铠确实为您所有。
t_en: Hold on, let me verify... Yep, that mechatron is indeed registered under your name.

t_ch: 好炫酷的机铠！我能开着它去战斗吗？
t_en: Whoa, this one's so cool! Can I pilot it into combat?

t_ch: 这…恐怕不行。驱动机铠需要驾驶员的愿力。破晓战队的定制机铠，都是他们亲自注入愿力培养的。
t_en: Well... I'm afraid that won't work. Operating a mechatron requires the pilot's Wishpower. And Daybreak Squadron's custom mechatrons were all personally nurtured by their own Wishpower.

t_ch: 你没有足够的愿力，又是个孩子，光是启动都很困难，更别说是战斗了。
t_en: You don't have enough Wishpower. Besides, you're just a kid. Even getting it started would be hard for you, let alone actual combat.

t_ch: 不要小看孩子的愿望啊！
t_en: Don't underestimate a kid's resolve!

t_ch: 让他试试吧。
t_en: Let's give him a shot.

t_ch: 是啊，说得对！总归要试试看！万一我成功了呢？
t_en: Exactly! At least let me have a shot. What if I actually pull it off?

t_ch: 好吧。我可以申请小型化流程，把它调取出来。你来试试，但别抱太大希望。
t_en: Alright. I can apply for the streamlined process and pull it up. Try your best, but don't get your hopes up.

t_ch: 我还有正经事要处理，就先失陪了。
t_en: I've got actual business to take care of, catch you later.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！这份恩情，我一辈子都不会忘记的！
t_en: Thank you so much, {F#miss}{M#sir}! I'll never forget you so long as I live!

t_ch: 你愿意开一辈子机铠吗？
t_en: Would you be willing to pilot a mechatron for the rest of your life?

t_ch: 当然！我要像破晓战队那样，做个驾驶机铠作战的英雄。永远也不会腻！
t_en: Of course! I wanna be a hero piloting mechatrons in battle, just like what Daybreak Squadron does. I can never get tired of it!

t_ch: 请叫我英雄。
t_en: You may call me a hero.

t_ch: 嗯！你就像灰烬那样，是个大英雄！我崇拜的对象！
t_en: Yeah! You're just like Cinderborne, a total hero! Someone I really look up to!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，有点期待呢~{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，我们快走吧！
t_en: Hehe, I'm so excited~ {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, shall we get going?

t_ch: 哇！这就是那台机铠吗？我、我该怎么做才行？
t_en: Whoa! Is that the mechatron!? Wh—What should I do?

t_ch: 认真地提供指导。
t_en: Provide serious guidance.

t_ch: 愿力是相信的力量。只要你认定自己能驾驶机铠，和破晓战士并肩作战，就真的能做到。
t_en: Wishpower's strength comes from belief. As long as you believe you can pilot a mechatron and fight alongside the Daybreak Warriors, you can make it happen.

t_ch: 传授灰烬的经验。
t_en: Impart Cinderborne's experience.

t_ch: 偶像的力量是无穷的。想象假如你是灰烬，你会怎么做。或许他的故事能给你力量。
t_en: The strength of an idol is infinite. Imagine if you were Cinderborne, what would you do? Perhaps his story can give you strength.

t_ch: 随便编点胡话。
t_en: Just make something up.

t_ch: 跟我念：即使命途兴衰消长，开拓者应自有主张…愿此行，终抵群星。
t_en: Repeat after me: No matter the ebb and flow of fate, stop staying within the lines... May this journey lead us starward.

t_ch: 啊，好像启动了！
t_en: Oh, looks like it's starting up!

t_ch: （这孩子居然真能操控机铠？我怕不是遇见了个小天才……）
t_en: (This kid can actually pilot a mechatron? I might've just stumbled upon a little prodigy here...)

t_ch: …站立！
t_en: ...Stand!

t_ch: 挥拳！
t_en: Swing!

t_ch: 成功了，我真的成功了！{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我驱动那台机铠了，就像破晓战士们一样！
t_en: I did it, I really did it! {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, I've piloted that mechatron, just like Daybreak Warriors did!

t_ch: 做得好，不愧是我教的。
t_en: Look at you go! Must be my influence.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，确实。如果没有{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}指点的话，我是不可能做到的。
t_en: Hehe, you got that right. I couldn't have done it without you showing me the ropes, {F#miss}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 你离梦想又近了一步。
t_en: You're one step closer to your dream.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，多亏{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}指点，不然只靠我是不可能做到的。
t_en: Hehe, all thanks to you for showing me the ropes, {F#miss}{M#sir}! No way I could've pulled it off on my own.

t_ch: 可能驱动机铠并没有那么难。
t_en: Maybe piloting a mechatron isn't as hard as it looks.

t_ch: 嗯…但没有{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}指点的话，只靠我是不可能做到的。
t_en: Hmm... But there's no way I could've done it on my own without your guidance, {F#miss}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 虽然幻想过好多次，可真到了这天，心脏还是会砰砰跳个不停……
t_en: I've dreamed about this day so many times, but now that it's actually here, my heart just won't stop pounding...

t_ch: 终于…终于可以向那个击败破晓战队的家伙，发起华丽的挑战了。
t_en: Finally... I can issue a spectacular challenge to the one who defeated Daybreak Squadron.

t_ch: 等着我啊——「球棒浣熊」！
t_en: Just you wait, Baseball Raccoon!

t_ch: 我成反派了？
t_en: Wait, I'm the villain now!?

t_ch: 等等…那不就是我吗？
t_en: Wait... isn't that me?

t_ch: 啊？你是说…你就是「球棒浣熊」？怎么可能，你根本不是浣熊啊！
t_en: Huh? You're saying... you're the Baseball Raccoon? How's that even possible!? You're not even a raccoon!

t_ch: 没想到我还是不够出名。
t_en: Guess I'm still not famous enough.

t_ch: 你没看过《苍天航路绒绒号》吗？
t_en: You've never seen Fluffy Across the Blue?

t_ch: 难道是我在医院躺太久，没看新闻的缘故？球棒浣熊，怎么是个人啊？！
t_en: Did I spend too long laid up in the hospital and miss the news or something? How is Baseball Raccoon a person!?

t_ch: 或许，这就是命运吧。出院后第一个帮助我的人，居然是我一直在找的仇敌……
t_en: Maybe that's just how it's meant to be.... The first person to help me after I got out of the hospital turned out to be the very enemy I'd been searching for all along...

t_ch: 抱歉了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，在幻月的见证下，我，宏树，要向你发起决斗！
t_en: Sorry, {F#miss}{M#sir}, but under the witness of Phantasmoon, I, Silas, challenge you to a duel!

t_ch: 给我个理由。
t_en: Give me a reason.

t_ch: 宿敌。这个理由够么？我们的命运注定要在此交汇，然后以华丽的决斗作为收尾。
t_en: You're my arch-nemesis. Is that enough? We are meant to clash here and end in a spectacular duel.

t_ch: 如此炽烈的情感，我接受了。
t_en: Such passionate emotions... I accept your challenge.

t_ch: 灰烬说过，情感是人类最为强大的力量。就把这次对战，当作你我信念的碰撞吧！
t_en: Cinderborne once said that emotions are humanity's greatest strength. So let this battle be a clash of our convictions!

t_ch: 但是，我拒绝！
t_en: I refuse!

t_ch: 直面我的怒火吧，宿敌。我有必须战胜你的理由，而你，没有逃跑的选择。
t_en: Then face my fury, arch-nemesis. I have my reasons for defeating you, and you have no choice but to stand and fight.

t_ch: 听说…这里即将展开一场激烈的机铠对战？
t_en: I heard... there's gonna be an intense mechatron showdown here?

t_ch: 没有观众的决斗是不完整的。因此，就由我来见证吧。绝对裁判得公正漂亮！
t_en: A duel without an audience is incomplete. Therefore, allow me to bear witness and be the arbitrator. I vow to judge fair and square!

t_ch: 杨叔怎么也刷新了？
t_en: Wait, where did Mr. Yang spawn from?

t_ch: 果然杨叔还是忘不了机器人。
t_en: As expected, Mr. Yang, the robot superfan.

t_ch: 杨叔也太入戏了…
t_en: Mr. Yang's really getting into character...

t_ch: 咳，先前我们是访客，总归要顾全礼数。
t_en: Ahem. Considering we were guests earlier, it was only proper to observe the courtesies.

t_ch: <size=28>但现在，我的机甲之魂在熊熊燃烧！</size>
t_en: <size=28>But now, my mech spirit burns brighter than ever!</size>

t_ch: 「球棒浣熊」同意了你的决斗邀请，现在，就请两位移步隔壁的战斗测试沙盒。
t_en: Baseball Raccoon has accepted your challenge. Now then, if you two would kindly proceed to the combat testing sandbox next door.

t_ch: 我不会退缩，堂堂正正决出胜负吧！
t_en: I won't back down! Let's settle this fair and square!

t_ch: 不错的宣言，年轻人。那么做好准备，去书写属于你们的传奇吧！
t_en: An admirable resolve, kid. Now steel yourselves and go forth to write your own legend!

t_ch: 「乐园历<unbreak>1999</unbreak>年，恐怖大王从天而降。{F#她}{M#他}行于毁灭的命途，誓要将乐园变成满是垃圾桶的世界。」
t_en: "In the year <unbreak>1999</unbreak> of the Arcadian Calendar, the King of Terror descended from the sky. {F#She}{M#He} walked the Path of Destruction, vowing to transform arcadia into a world overflowing with trashcans."

t_ch: 「二维市的守护者——破晓战队前去阻拦，而{F#她}{M#他}只是轻轻挥动球棒，就将破晓战士们尽数击败。」
t_en: "Duomension City's guardian, Daybreak Squadron, tried to stop {F#her}{M#him}, but with just a casual swing of {F#her}{M#his} bat, {F#she}{M#he} defeated every last Daybreak Warrior."

t_ch: 「恐怖大王」，对观众们说点什么吧。
t_en: King of Terror, say something to the audience, will you?

t_ch: 这解说词是谁写的…
t_en: Who wrote this narration...

t_ch: 我在网上找了个模板，根据做动画师的经验做了几处改编，希望各位喜欢。
t_en: I found a template online and made a few tweaks based on my experience as an animator. Hope you'll like it.

t_ch: 为什么我不是守护者？
t_en: Why am I not a guardian?

t_ch: 反派也有反派的救世主，这场战争无关乎善恶，而是宿命般的对决。
t_en: Even villains have their saviors. This war isn't about good and evil. It's a battle that was always meant to happen.

t_ch: 「二维市最后的防线已被攻破。在生死存亡的时刻，破晓战队的后继者——名为宏树的少年决定挺身而出。」
t_en: "Duomension City's final line of defense has been breached. At this critical moment, a boy named Silas, successor to the Daybreak Squadron, decides to stand and fight."

t_ch: 说点什么吧，被世界选中的少年。
t_en: Care to say something, Chosen One?

t_ch: 呃，那就…我不会逃避，这是属于我的战斗！
t_en: Uh, well then... I won't back down. This is my fight!

t_ch: 英雄与恐怖大王都已就位。我宣布——破晓战队的终局之战，即将揭幕！
t_en: The hero and the King of Terror have taken their positions. I hereby declare: The Final Battle of Daybreak Squadron is about to commence!

t_ch: 咳咳…机铠，被击碎了？好不容易才拿到手的……
t_en: *cough*... My mechatron is... smashed to pieces? But I just got my hands on it...

t_ch: 我…输了吗？就像破晓战队那样？
t_en: I... lost? Just like Daybreak Squadron?

t_ch: 我是不是下手太狠了？
t_en: Did I go too hard on this kid?

t_ch: 不，是我太弱了，真正的英雄不会被打倒。果然，我离英雄还是太遥远了……
t_en: No, it's my problem. I'm just too weak. A true hero would never be defeated. I guess... I'm still far from being a hero...

t_ch: 你已经尽力了。
t_en: You did your best.

t_ch: 不，输了就是输了。真正的英雄不会被打倒。果然，我离英雄还是太遥远了……
t_en: No, a loss is a loss. A true hero would never be defeated. I guess... I'm still far from being a hero...

t_ch: 大错特错！
t_en: Dead wrong!

t_ch: 孩子，你把英雄想得太厉害了。英雄不是神，英雄会失败，也会迷茫，更会遭受挫折……
t_en: Kid, you're giving heroes way too much credit. Heroes aren't gods. They fail, they lose their way, and they face setbacks just like anyone else...

t_ch: 但英雄之所以被称为英雄，是因为他们总能重新站起来。所以，站起来吧，少年！
t_en: But the reason we call them heroes is that they always get back up. So get up, kid!

t_ch: 灰烬…是真的灰烬战士吗？请与我合影！
t_en: Cinderborne... Is that really you? Can I get a photo with you!?

t_ch: 不，现在的我只是个观众。代表破晓战队的，是那个孩子才对。
t_en: No, I'm just a spectator. The one representing Daybreak Squadron should be that kid.

t_ch: 看啊，少年，你背负着破晓战队的名号，不要辜负那些支持你的人，站起来。
t_en: Look, kid, you carry the name of Daybreak Squadron on your shoulders. Don't let down the people who believe in you. Now get up.

t_ch: 然后…向那不讲理的命运，吹声口哨，挥出重拳吧！
t_en: Then... whistle at that unjust destiny and hit it with everything you've got!

t_ch: 支持我？真的…有人支持我吗？
t_en: Is this... real? People are actually supporting me?

t_ch: 站起来！别给战队粉丝丢脸！让那些观众见识下我们的厉害！
t_en: Get up! Don't let us Daybreak Squadron fans down! Show those spectators what we're made of!

t_ch: 是啊，真正的英雄才不会被打倒，上啊！我支持你！
t_en: That's right, a true hero never stays down! Go get 'em! I've got your back!

t_ch: 站起来，你的战斗还没结束。
t_en: Get up. Your fight isn't over yet.

t_ch: 光靠意志，可没法击败我。
t_en: Willpower alone won't be enough to beat me.

t_ch: 耳边萦绕的声音，那些助威和呼喊…原来，我也能像英雄一样背负人们的期待吗。
t_en: The voices echoing in my ears, all those cheers and shouts... So even I can carry people's hopes and expectations, just like a hero would.

t_ch: <size=36>力量，涌进来了！</size>
t_en: <size=36>The power... it's pouring into me!</size>

t_ch: 竟然有这种事！人们的愿力重塑了宏树破损的机铠。他还没有倒下！
t_en: Incredible! The people's Wishpower has mended Silas's damaged mechatron. He's still standing!

t_ch: <size=36>再战一场吧，球棒浣熊！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Let's go for another round, Baseball Raccoon!</size>

t_ch: （还真是不服输啊。气氛都推到这了，后续的战斗…我还要使出全力吗？）
t_en: (Man, this kid really doesn't know when to quit. The tension's already through the roof... Do I really need to go all-out in this fight?)

t_ch: （故意放水，让宏树获胜）
t_en: (Deliberately hold back and let Silas win)

t_ch: （也不是什么事关银河存亡的战斗，就别太认真了吧。）
t_en: (It's not like the fate of the cosmos hangs in the balance here. No need to get so worked up about it.)

t_ch: （英雄守护的不仅是世界，更是人们的愿望。我…想守护宏树的梦想。）
t_en: (Being a hero isn't just about saving the world. It's about protecting what people believe in. And I... I want to safeguard Silas's dream.)

t_ch: （必须要全力以赴）
t_en: (Gotta give it everything I've got)

t_ch: （胜利不是靠施舍得来的。想成为英雄，必须靠自己的双手争取才行。）
t_en: (Victory isn't something you get handed to you on a silver platter. If you wanna be a hero, you've gotta earn it with your own two hands.)

t_ch: （就用这饱含全部力量的一击，向你表示敬意吧，宏树！）
t_en: (Then let me show you my respect with a strike backed by everything I've got, Silas!)

t_ch: 在你的狂轰滥炸下，重燃起战意的宏树迅速倒地了。
t_en: Under your relentless barrage, Silas's rekindled fighting spirit was quickly snuffed out.

t_ch: 咳…果然还是…做不到吗……
t_en: *cough*... Guess I... still can't do it...

t_ch: 我输了，你真的很强…破晓战队败在你手上，我心服口服。
t_en: I lost... I'm no match for you. You beat Daybreak Squadron fair and square... Can't argue with that.

t_ch: 曾经有位少年，下定决心要成为拯救世界的英雄，但球棒浣熊无情地粉碎了他的梦想。
t_en: Once upon a time, there was a boy who resolved to become a hero who would save the world. But in the end, Baseball Raccoon mercilessly crushed his dreams.

t_ch: 许多年后，白发苍苍的他坐在窗边，回首往事，是否还会记得年幼时许下的愿望？
t_en: Would the boy still remember the wish he made as a child many years later, when he sat by the window, his hair white, reminiscing about the past...?

t_ch: 是不是有点太沉重了…
t_en: Isn't this a bit too dark...?

t_ch: 哈…哈哈哈！谢谢你…愿意陪我胡闹，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。
t_en: Ha... hahahaha! Thank you... for playing along with me, {F#miss}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 其实，我知道的，不管是输是赢，已经发生的事都不会有任何改变。
t_en: Truth is, I know that no matter if I win or lose, what's already happened won't change.

t_ch: 我只是…想成为灰烬那样的英雄罢了。
t_en: I just... wanted to be a hero like Cinderborne, that's all.

t_ch: （说出对灰烬说过的台词）
t_en: (Repeat what was once said to Cinderborne)

t_ch: 有句话，我对灰烬说过，现在也同样送给你。
t_en: There's something I once said to Cinderborne, and now I'm passing it on to you.

t_ch: ——每个在自己的生命里坚持下去的人，都是英雄。
t_en: Anyone who perseveres in their own life is a hero indeed!

t_ch: 嗯。我会记住的。
t_en: Yeah. I'll keep that in mind.

t_ch: 对了，那位灰烬战士呢？我还有好多话想对他说。
t_en: Oh, speaking of which, where's that Cinderborne warrior? I still have so much I want to say to him.

t_ch: 在一场堪称影帝级别的打戏后，你恰到好处地倒下了。
t_en: After an award-worthy fight scene, you collapse at just the right moment.

t_ch: 好、好厉害，我不是对手…你成功击败了我，我认输，真的……
t_en: W—Wow, you're too strong... I'm no match for you... You beat me, fair and square. I give up, I really do...

t_ch: 这…我做到了吗？我…赢了！
t_en: I... I did it? I... I won!

t_ch: 一番恶战过后，破晓战队的后继者成功守护了城市。让我们为这位少年献上掌声！
t_en: After a fierce battle, the successor of Daybreak Squadron successfully protected the city. Let us give this young hero a round of applause!

t_ch: 我…赢了？{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我真的做到了！
t_en: I... I won? I really did it, {F#miss}{M#sir}!

t_ch: 其实，我知道的，不管是输是赢，已经发生的事都不会有任何改变。
t_en: Truth is, I know that no matter if I win or lose, what's already happened won't change.

t_ch: 但当英雄的感觉，真的很不错。谢谢你愿意陪我胡闹！
t_en: But being the hero feels so great! Thanks for playing along with me!

t_ch: （说出对灰烬说过的台词）
t_en: (Repeat what was once said to Cinderborne)

t_ch: 有句话，我对灰烬说过，现在也同样送给你。
t_en: There's something I once said to Cinderborne, and now I'm passing it on to you.

t_ch: ——每个在自己的生命里坚持下去的人，都是英雄。
t_en: Anyone who perseveres in their own life is a hero indeed!

t_ch: 嗯。我会记住的！
t_en: Yeah. I'll keep that in mind!

t_ch: 对了，那位灰烬战士呢？我还有好多话想对他说！
t_en: Oh, speaking of which, where's that Cinderborne warrior? I still have so much I want to say to him!

t_ch: 请等一下！我…你还记得我吗？我是宏树，我们在第三医院见过，那是三年前的事……
t_en: Wait just a second! I... Do you still remember me? I'm Silas. We once met at the Third Hospital three years ago...

t_ch: 你可能不记得了，但我一直记得你对我说过的话！是你支撑着我走到了现在。看，我现在已经能跑能跳了，还能驱动机铠！
t_en: You might not remember it, but I've never forgotten what you said to me! Your words kept me going all this time. Look at me now! I can run, I can jump, and I can even pilot a mechatron!

t_ch: 我会继续磨炼战斗技巧，争取成为像你一样的英雄！我真的想做破晓战队的后继者！
t_en: I'll keep honing my combat skills and work to become a hero just like you! I really wanna be a successor to Daybreak Squadron!

t_ch: 你是个好孩子，而我不够格做你的偶像。真实的我，并不像这套战服那般耀眼。
t_en: You're doing great, kid. But I'm not worthy to be your idol. The real me... isn't nearly as dazzling as this suit.

t_ch: 但超级英雄萨尔瓦多曾说过，「即使只是追逐幻月的影子，我们走过的路也是真实的。」
t_en: But as Salvador the superhero once said, "Even if we're only chasing the shadow of Phantasmoon, the path we walk is real."

t_ch: 保持这份斗志吧，或许在未来，你会成为真正的英雄。我期待着那天。
t_en: Keep that fighting spirit alive. Who knows? Maybe one day you'll become a true hero. I'll be looking forward to it.

t_ch: 等等，我还有问题要问！
t_en: Wait, I have some questions to ask!

t_ch: …你是真弘吗？
t_en: ...Are you Magnus?

t_ch: 无视你的阻拦，灰烬迅速消失在人群中。
t_en: Ignoring your attempts to stop him, Cinderborne quickly vanishes into the crowd.

t_ch: 呼…也到我该退场的时候了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。我会永远记住这次经历的。
t_en: Phew... Looks like it's time for me to leave, {F#miss}{M#sir}. I'll never forget what we had today.

t_ch: 我有份临别礼物给你。
t_en: I've got a parting gift for you.

t_ch: 这是「帕姆王-合金武装<unbreak>999</unbreak>」，破晓战队就是驾驶着它…被打败的。
t_en: This is the "King Pom-Pom: Alloy Armor <unbreak>999</unbreak>," the very mech Daybreak Squadron piloted... to their defeat.

t_ch: 现在，我把它送给你。希望我，还有星穹列车，也能成为你心中的英雄。
t_en: Now, I'm giving it to you. I hope that the Astral Express and I can become heroes in your heart too.

t_ch: 嗯！
t_en: You bet!

t_ch: 再见吧，未来的英雄。
t_en: Farewell, hero of the future generation.

t_ch: 再见了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，希望我们…都能实现各自的梦想！
t_en: Goodbye, {F#miss}{M#sir}! I hope we can... both make our dreams come true!

t_ch: （不管怎样，我至少为宏树圆了驾驶机铠的梦。）
t_en: (Anyway, at least I helped Silas live out his dream of piloting a mechatron.)

t_ch: （刚才那个灰烬…是真弘吗？还好留了他的联系方式，去问问吧。）
t_en: (Was that Cinderborne just now... Magnus? Good thing I got his contact info. Better go ask him about it.)

t_ch: 是你啊，前辈…有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh, it's you, {M#Mr. Trailblazer}{F#Miss Trailblazer}... How can I help?

t_ch: 有个孩子，继承了你的意志。
t_en: There's a kid who's carrying on your legacy.

t_ch: 你现在也是前辈了。
t_en: You've got someone looking up to you now.

t_ch: 你向真弘讲述了宏树的故事，以及他决心成为破晓战队后继者的信念。
t_en: You told Magnus about Silas and his unwavering resolve to become the successor to Daybreak Squadron.

t_ch: 哈哈，没想到我还有这么热血的粉丝。也好，这个世界需要英雄，永远都需要。
t_en: Haha, didn't think I'd have such a passionate fan. That's good. After all, this world is always in need of heroes.

t_ch: 自从那件事结束后，我就一直在医院卧床休息。就连到隔壁串门，都得被医生唠叨半天，更别说溜出去了。
t_en: Ever since that whole incident wrapped up, I've been stuck in bed resting at the hospital. Even popping next door gets me a lecture from the doctor, let alone sneaking out.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>你们见到的，一定是真正的灰烬战士吧。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>What you encountered must have been the real Cinderborne.</color>

t_ch: 嗯，我猜就是这样。回见了，前辈。
t_en: Well, I guess that's it. See you around, {M#Mr. Trailblazer}{F#Miss Trailblazer}.

t_ch: （这孩子这么热衷于机铠，肯定会很喜欢限定款模型吧。）
t_en: (Since this kid is so into mechatrons, I bet they'd love a limited edition model.)

t_ch: 送出「帕姆王」。
t_en: Give "King Pom-Pom."

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's reconsider.

t_ch: 咕咕——这群调皮孩子，一个个全跑没影了。
t_en: Hoot hoot... These playful children, they've all gone missing.

t_ch: 这下可伤脑筋了咕。
t_en: What a headache, hoot.

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 咕咕——是你呀，了不起的新同学。
t_en: Hoot hoot... It's you, the distinguished new student.

t_ch: 今天本该是折纸树林期末测验的日子，没想到这群孩子听到考试，趁老鸟不注意一溜烟全飞走了，咕咕咕。
t_en: Today was supposed to be Paperfold Woods's final exam day, but once those children heard about the exam, they all fled without a trace while I wasn't looking, hoot hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕——以前没见过你，是新来的转校生吗？
t_en: Hoot hoot... I've never seen you before. Are you a new transfer student?

t_ch: 很抱歉，我这儿出了点意外，暂时抽不出空帮你办理入学手续咕。
t_en: My apologies, I'm in a bit of trouble here — I won't be able to help you handle your admission process right now.

t_ch: 今天本该是折纸树林期末测验的日子，没想到这群孩子听到考试，趁老鸟不注意，一溜烟全飞走了，咕咕咕！
t_en: Today was supposed to be Paperfold Woods's final exam day, but once those children heard about the exam, they all fled without a trace while I wasn't looking, hoot hoot!

t_ch: 期末测验？
t_en: Final exam?

t_ch: 咕咕，老鸟主张寓教于乐，让孩子们在游戏与实践中学习——随着小鸟们逐渐成长，我会慢慢增添书面测验这种更加严谨的教学方式。
t_en: Hoot hoot, I advocate in learning through play, so the children study through games and practical tasks. As the birds grow, I will slowly add stringent educational methods like textbook tests.

t_ch: 可这群小家伙生性向往自由，抗议道美梦小镇中不该有考试这般邪恶的存在咕。
t_en: But these lil' fellas inherently yearn for freedom, and protest these tests — an evil presence they deem alien in the Dreamville, hoot.

t_ch: 老鸟刚去找过一圈，它们个个看见我调头就飞，我这老鸟扇翅膀的速度也比不上它们……一趟下来腰酸背痛不说，孩子们一个也没带回来咕。
t_en: I just got done searching for a round — they turn tail and fly away the moment they see me. These old wings of mine are no match for them in speed... Not only is my back sore now, I didn't even manage to retrieve a little birdie, hoot.

t_ch: 不过，咕咕——新同学，我看你腿脚利索，人也很亲切——小鸟们一定会信任你的，不如来做我的帮手吧。
t_en: But, hoot hoot... New student, I see you're quick on your feet, and you're affable — the birds will surely trust you. How about helping me out?

t_ch: 这个我擅长。
t_en: This is my specialty.

t_ch: 需要我做什么直说吧。
t_en: Just let me know what you need.

t_ch: 必须帮，考试是鸟生大事。
t_en: Count me in. Exams are a major event in a bird's life.

t_ch: 咕咕，真是乐于助人的同学！那么，替老鸟去找找顽皮的孩子们吧，也帮我捎句话——等考试结束，老鸟会带它们去镇上买好吃的美梦脆筒，决不食言咕！
t_en: Hoot hoot, such a helpful student! Well then, look for those mischievous children in my stead, and pass along a message: When exams are over, this old bird will take them to the town and buy tasty Dreamy Cones! Pinky promise, hoot!

t_ch: 老鸟会在这里守着回来的小鸟，寻找的事就拜托你了！事后老鸟必有重谢咕。
t_en: I will stay here and wait for the returning birds. I'm leaving the search to you! This old bird will surely repay you lavishly, hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕——
t_en: Hoot hoot...

t_ch: 1…2…3…已经劝回了这么多孩子。
t_en: 1... 2... 3... Already so many children persuaded to come home.

t_ch: 我就知道小鸟们喜欢你。这是老鸟准备的一点谢礼，收下吧。
t_en: I knew the birds liked you. This is a token of appreciation from this old bird. Please take it.

t_ch: 咕咕咕——
t_en: Hoot hoot hoot...

t_ch: 咕！孩子们全乖乖回来了！
t_en: Hoot hoot! The children have all obediently returned!

t_ch: 了不起的新同学，谢谢你对折纸树林教育的助力，请务必收下老鸟的心意。
t_en: Distinguished new student, thank you for assisting Paperfold Woods' educational process. You must accept my token of appreciation.

t_ch: 咕咕——慢慢来，不用着急，我会等到所有孩子回来再开始考试。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Slow does it, and there's no rush. I will wait for all the children to return before starting the exam.

t_ch: 咕咕——孩子们都跟着你回来了，它们果然很信任你。
t_en: Hoot hoot... The children have all returned with you. They really trust you so much.

t_ch: 麻烦新同学了，这是老鸟的一点心意，请收下咕。
t_en: Thank you for your trouble, new student. This is a token of this old bird's appreciation. Please accept it, hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕——太好了，孩子们全都乖乖回来了！可以开始考试了！
t_en: Hoot hoot... Excellent, all the children have obediently returned! We can start the exam!

t_ch: 了不起的新同学，谢谢你对折纸树林教育的助力，请务必收下老鸟的心意。
t_en: Distinguished new student, thank you for assisting Paperfold Woods' educational process. You must accept my token of appreciation.

t_ch: 咕咕——太好了，孩子们全都乖乖回来了！
t_en: Hoot hoot... Excellent, all the children have obediently returned!

t_ch: 了不起的新同学，谢谢你对折纸树林教育的助力，请务必收下老鸟的心意。
t_en: Distinguished new student, thank you for assisting Paperfold Woods' educational process. You must accept my token of appreciation.

t_ch: 咕咕——了不起的新同学，有空可以加入我们的课堂，小鸟们见到你会开心的。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Distinguished new student, join our classes if you have time. The birds will be happy to see you.

t_ch: 以后再说吧。
t_en: Catch you next time.

t_ch: 咕咕，这些小调皮就麻烦你了。
t_en: Hoot hoot, I'll be leaving these little rascals to you.

t_ch: 咕咕，随时欢迎你来折纸树林上课。
t_en: Hoot hoot, you are always welcome to attend class in Paperfold Woods.

t_ch: 关于你自己…
t_en: About yourself...

t_ch: 我是「猫头鹰老师」，帮一群厉害的朋友们建造了美梦小镇咕。
t_en: I am Professor Owl. I helped a group of amazing friends build Dreamville, hoot.

t_ch: ——不过，那都是很多年前的事了。现在老鸟是折纸小鸟们的老师。毕竟鸟的心力总有耗尽的时候，要将未来的天空交给下一代才行咕。
t_en: ...But, that was many, many years ago. I am now the origami birds' teacher. A bird's energy and spirit do whittle eventually — we must entrust the skies of tomorrow to the next generation, hoot.

t_ch: 关于折纸树林…
t_en: About Paperfold Woods...

t_ch: 咕咕！折纸树林是老鸟最骄傲的作品咕！
t_en: Hoot hoot! Paperfold Woods is this old bird's proudest creation, hoot!

t_ch: 为孩子们埋下理想的种子是老师的责任——从第一个创办的积木课堂，到后续开设的音符课堂、金币课堂，希望每只小鸟都能找到属于自己的美梦咕。
t_en: It is a teacher's duty to plant the seeds of dreams for the children — from the inaugural building block class to the subsequent music note classes and Gold Coin Classes. I hope that every bird will be able to find their own beautiful dream, hoot.

t_ch: 我需要一些提示。
t_en: I need a few hints.

t_ch: 咕咕——年纪大了，脑子也不好使了。
t_en: Hoot hoot... The mind gets hazier with age.

t_ch: 若是能服用一粒「魔法硬币」，我就能想起更多细节咕。
t_en: If I could imbibe a magic coin, I could probably recall more details, hoot.

t_ch: （拿出一枚艾迪恩代币）
t_en: (Take out an Aideen Token)

t_ch: 咕咕！新生里果然卧虎藏龙。
t_en: Hoot hoot! There are indeed hidden talents among new students.

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师优雅地叼过你拿出的代币，然后…吃掉了它！
t_en: Professor Owl augustly takes the token from you with his beak, and then... swallows it!

t_ch: 咳咳——真是良药苦口。
t_en: *cough* Truly a bitter medicine.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个热心肠的孩子，总爱揽下所有接待新生的活…估计它在<color=#dbc291ff>学校大门口</color>忙着呢，去看看吧！
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a warm-hearted kid, who loves to take on the responsibility of welcoming new students... I bet they're busy at the <color=#dbc291ff>academy's entrance</color>. Go take a look!

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个灵力很强的孩子似乎能看见未来，或许它又在<color=#dbc291ff>某个指示路牌上</color>替人占卜呢。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child with strong psychic powers who seems to be able to foretell the future. Maybe they're at <color=#dbc291ff>some signpost</color>, helping others with divination again.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个孩子遇到了青春的烦恼，正四处寻觅漂亮戒指，想赠予心仪的小鸟…你去有<color=#dbc291ff>戒指</color>的地方找找吧，也劝劝他这并非真爱所需咕。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's troubled by the growing pains of youth, searching everywhere for a beautiful ring to gift to their beloved little bird... Go look for them where the <color=#dbc291ff>rings</color> are, and maybe remind him that true love does not need such things, hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个乐于分享知识的孩子，总带着它的小黑板在树林里给人补习…多留意下路过见到的<color=#dbc291ff>小黑板</color>吧。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who loves sharing knowledge, always carrying a small blackboard to tutor others... Keep an eye out for any <color=#dbc291ff>small blackboards</color> you might pass by.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个向往职场生活的孩子，最近经常假借别人的<color=#dbc291ff>画像</color>扮演人事玩招聘呢，或许它又跑到那附近去了。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who longs for the working life. Recently, they've been borrowing other people's <color=#dbc291ff>portraits</color>, posing as HR and role-playing a recruiter. Maybe they have run off somewhere nearby.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个狂热喜欢气球的孩子，或许正躲藏在<color=#dbc291ff>某个水池上方的气球</color>中呢，快去找它吧。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's crazy about balloons. Maybe they're hiding inside <color=#dbc291ff>some balloon above a certain pool</color>. Go find it quickly.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个热爱哲学的孩子，爱找人进行激烈的辩论，或许它正躲在<color=#dbc291ff>某个花瓶</color>中准备伏击过往的路人呢。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who loves philosophy and debating with others. Maybe it's hiding in <color=#dbc291ff>some vase</color>, waiting to ambush passersby.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个爱跳舞的孩子，老和<color=#dbc291ff>跳舞的人偶</color>待在一起，要陪它跳一曲吗？
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who loves to dance, and they're always with the <color=#dbc291ff>dancing puppets</color>. Care to dance with it?

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个梦想着变得金光闪闪的孩子，总仰望着折纸树林里<color=#dbc291ff>最闪闪发光的大鸟</color>，寻找它要抬头看咕。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who dreams to become all shiny and dazzling, and it's always dreaming of becoming the <color=#dbc291ff>most radiant, biggest bird</color> in Paperfold Woods. To look for it, one must raise one's head, hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个很黏我的孩子，平日里与我寸步不离，搞不好就在我的附近，你应该不难发现它的身影咕。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's very attached to me and never leaves my side. They might be nearby now, and you can't miss it, hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个喜欢游泳的孩子，走到哪儿游到哪儿，在校园的<color=#dbc291ff>水池</color>中找找它的身影吧。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who loves to swim — it swims wherever it goes. Look for it at the campus <color=#dbc291ff>pool</color>.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个方向感很强的孩子，或许又在<color=#dbc291ff>铺满箭头的灯箱</color>上蹲着给人指路呢。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's great at directions. Maybe they're crouching at <color=#dbc291ff>a lightbox covered in arrows</color>, helping others with directions.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个孩子在准备一场惊喜生日派对，或许你可以在<color=#dbc291ff>飘浮起的礼盒</color>中寻找它的身影咕。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child planning for a surprise birthday party. Maybe you can find them near those <color=#dbc291ff>floating bundles</color>, hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个孩子对颜色非常敏锐，它或许在<color=#dbc291ff>彩色的旗帜上</color>盘算着颜色搭配呢。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's really sensitive to colors. Maybe they're at the <color=#dbc291ff>colorful flags</color>, calculating color combinations.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个孩子对财富管理十分有心得，或许它就在<color=#dbc291ff>摆放着金币的摊位</color>挑选自己下一款理财产品呢。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's very knowledgeable about wealth management. Maybe they're at a <color=#dbc291ff>stall with gold coins</color>, choosing their next financial product.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个上课爱打瞌睡的孩子，路过<color=#dbc291ff>校园的教室</color>时不要忘了看看它在不在。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who loves sleeping during classes. If you ever pass by any <color=#dbc291ff>classrooms</color>, don't forget to see if they're around.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个在等人的孩子，我猜它和朋友约定在<color=#dbc291ff>某个教室</color>门口相见咕。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's waiting for someone. I'm guessing they have arranged to meet their friend at the entrance of <color=#dbc291ff>some classroom</color>, hoot.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个很受欢迎的孩子，这会儿或许在<color=#dbc291ff>舞台</color>进行音乐表演呢，不知道它演奏的曲子是否合你的口味咕？
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a very popular child who might be performing on <color=#dbc291ff>stage</color> right now. I wonder if the piece they're playing will suit your taste, hoot?

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个将追求刺激贯彻到底的孩子，或许又在<color=#dbc291ff>舞台附近的喷气口</color>玩着蹦极呢。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who's devoted to seeking thrills. Maybe they're bungee jumping near the <color=#dbc291ff>stage's air vents</color>.

t_ch: 咕咕…噢！有个喜欢天文学的孩子，它搞不好又蹲在<color=#dbc291ff>某处的星球仪</color>上呢。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a child who loves astronomy. They might be crouched over <color=#dbc291ff>some armillary sphere</color>.

t_ch: 咕咕——还好有你出手相助，不然老鸟真拿孩子们没办法。
t_en: Hoot hoot, thank you for helping me out, else this old bird would be in knots trying to deal with these kids.

t_ch: 我会继续回忆其他孩子的去处，就拜托你把刚才提到的小调皮带回来咕。
t_en: I'll try to recall where the other kids went. I'm counting on you to bring back these playful ones, hoot.

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 没关系，老鸟再敲打敲打这生锈的脑袋咕。
t_en: No worries. This old bird will try to jog his memory by rattling this rusty brain, hoot.

t_ch: 关于你的学生…
t_en: What about your apprentices...

t_ch: 对这个毫无预兆的问题，猫头鹰老师显得有些诧异——他清了清嗓子，快速整理好了表情。
t_en: Professor Owl is obviously somewhat puzzled at this sudden question — he clears his throat, and hastily composes himself.

t_ch: 咕咕，你已经在其他地方见过它们了，还有什么好奇的吗？
t_en: Cuckoo, you've already seen them in other places. Why the curiosity?

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师，猫头鹰老师啾——
t_en: Professor Owl, Professor Owl, tweet...

t_ch: 耳边传来一阵啾啾喳喳的吵闹声，五个圆滚滚的身影扑哧着翅膀飞了过来。
t_en: A chatter of cacophony reaches your ears — five rotund figures fly over with their flappy wings.

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师，我是你的学生湛蓝爵士啾——
t_en: Professor Owl, I'm your student, Dr. Blues, tweet!

t_ch: 不对啾！！！我们说好了，按照颜色分配——你是妃色夫人，我才是湛蓝爵士啾。
t_en: That's not right, tweet!!! We agreed to play our roles according to color — you're Lady Vermilion, and I should be Dr. Blues, tweet.

t_ch: 可是人家最喜欢湛蓝爵士的作品啾，就要当湛蓝爵士啾！
t_en: But I love Dr. Blues' pieces the most, tweet. I wanna be Dr. Blues, tweet!

t_ch: 你们在干什么？
t_en: What are you all doing?

t_ch: 啾啾，所有小鸟列队重来一遍，我来起头…我们是猫头鹰老师引以为傲的学生——
t_en: Tweet, tweet, all birds line up and start over, I'll take the lead... We're the students that Professor Owl is most proud of...

t_ch: ——传说中最优秀的五只折纸小鸟啾！！！
t_en: ...The most outstanding five origami birds of legend, tweeeet!!!

t_ch: 咕咕……
t_en: Hoot hoot...

t_ch: 又来了，又来了，老师又露出那种表情了啾！
t_en: He's doing it again. Professor's making that face again, tweet!

t_ch: 哪种表情？
t_en: What face?

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong with Professor Owl?

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师最爱讲这五只折纸小鸟的故事：黄金公子、赤红大哥、草绿经理、妃色夫人、湛蓝爵士…所有小鸟都是听着这些名字长大的啾。每次提到他们，老师都可骄傲了啾。
t_en: Professor Owl loves telling the story of the five origami birds: Young Master Gold, Boss Red, Verdant Manager, Lady Vermilion, and Dr. Blues... All the birds grew up hearing these names, tweet. Each time they are mentioned, the Professor's always beams with pride.

t_ch: 但…每次讲完，老师又会躲着大家，在一旁偷偷叹气，我们都发现了啾。
t_en: But... after finishing the story, he just avoids everyone again, hiding in a corner and sighing in secret. We've all noticed it, tweet.

t_ch: 一定是前辈们太忙了，都没时间回树林探望猫头鹰老师，所以才让老师这么难过啾！
t_en: It must be because our seniors are too busy to come back and visit Professor Owl. That's why he's all sad, tweet!

t_ch: 所以——我们五个扮成了前辈们的样子，这样老师就不会寂寞了啾啾！
t_en: So... us five dressed up as our seniors. Then the Professor won't be so lonely, tweet tweet!

t_ch: 本来一切都很完美的…都怪小粉你一开始就说错名字！都被你搞砸啦啾啾啾！
t_en: Everything was supposed to be perfect... You're to blame, Pinkie, for saying the wrong name at the start! It's all your fault, tweet tweet tweet!

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师眼中流露出笑意，他扇着翅膀落到了小鸟们身边，逐个摸了摸孩子们古灵精怪的小脑瓜……
t_en: Professor Owl's eyes reveal an amused glint, as he flaps his wings and lands beside the birds, patting the children's mischievous little heads in turn...

t_ch: 咕咕——小鸟要和和气气，不要吵架喔。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Birds must get along now. Don't fight.

t_ch: 你们的心意，猫头鹰老师接收到了。谢谢你们咕……
t_en: Professor Owl appreciates your sentiment. Thank you all, hoot...

t_ch: 啾啾，猫头鹰老师，我们真的可以成为和前辈们一样厉害的折纸小鸟吗？
t_en: Tweet tweet, Professor Owl, can we really become awesome origami birds like our seniors?

t_ch: 咕咕——当然可以，老师相信你们。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Of course, I believe in you all.

t_ch: 老师，我的算术老是不及格啾，也可以像黄金公子那样…设计出金光闪闪的广场吗？
t_en: Professor, I keep failing math, tweet. Can I still be like Young Master Gold... and design a golden, shiny plaza?

t_ch: 咕咕——只要你在算术课上别再睡觉就好。
t_en: Hoot hoot... As long as you stop dozing off in math class.

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师，等我成了前辈们那样厉害的折纸小鸟…美梦小镇会记得我的名字吗啾？
t_en: Professor Owl, when I become as strong an origami bird as my seniors... Will Dreamville remember my name, tweet?

t_ch: 小鸟望向猫头鹰老师——天真的孩子眨着一双双圆溜溜的大眼睛，期盼着最信赖的老师许诺中那无所不能的未来。
t_en: The bird looks in Professor Owl's direction. The innocent child blinks with its round, limpid eyes, eagerly anticipating the almighty future that the most trusted teacher promised.
Hoot...

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师张开翅膀，紧紧抱住了他的学生，像是把年幼的孩子们牢牢护在羽翼之下，连同他们襁褓中的美梦……
t_en: Professor Owl spreads his wings and tightly embraces his dear students, as if protecting the young children within his wings, along with the sweet dreams cradled in their hearts...

t_ch: …啾？猫头鹰老师？
t_en: ...Tweet? Professor Owl?

t_ch: 没问题的！绝对会的！老鸟向你们保证咕！
t_en: Of course! They absolutely will! This old bird promises you, hoot!

t_ch: 啾——猫头鹰老师最好了！！！
t_en: Tweet... Professor Owl is the best!!!

t_ch: 咕咕——所以，孩子们，谁想去小镇里吃好吃的美梦脆筒？
t_en: Hoot hoot... Now, children, who wants to go to town for some Dreamy Cones?

t_ch: 我要我要我要，啾啾啾！！！
t_en: Me me me, tweet tweet tweet!!!

t_ch: 那就…先乖乖参加期末考咕！
t_en: Then... be good and sit for your final exam first, hoot!

t_ch: 啾啾啾啾啾！！!
t_en: Tweet tweet tweet tweet tweet!!!

t_ch: 考完才给吃，猫头鹰老师真狡猾啾！
t_en: We only get to eat after the exam? Professor Owl's so cunning, tweet!

t_ch: 咕咕——好好学习，可是成为厉害的折纸小鸟的第一步哦。
t_en: Hoot hoot... Study hard. That's the first step to becoming a strong origami bird.

t_ch: 阿德勒_日常
t_en: Adler_ daily

t_ch: 又见面了，最近你有没有发现什么新奇的小生物呢？
t_en: We meet again. Did you find any new creatures recently?

t_ch: 你在研究什么生物呢？
t_en: What creatures do you study?

t_ch: 当然还是在围绕珍稀物种领域啦。那些小家伙们可比你们这种大人有意思多了！
t_en: Rare species of course. Those little creatures are so much more interesting than human grown-ups!

t_ch: 不过……看你的眼神，似乎很好奇的样子。这种眼神可在大人里不多见了。
t_en: However... the curiosity in your eyes... I don't see that a lot in grown-ups' eyes.

t_ch: 你有什么想问的，就尽管问吧。只要是我知道的，都会为你解答的。
t_en: Let me know if you have any questions. I'll tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 你最喜欢什么珍稀物种
t_en: What is your favorite rare species?

t_ch: 作为研究者，我对于珍稀物种都是平等看待的，不会有喜恶之别。
t_en: As a researcher, I treat every rare species equally. I don't have a preference.

t_ch: 但是从个人角度的话……我最喜欢的是呜呜伯。
t_en: But from my personal perspective... I'd say Wubbaboos are my favorite.

t_ch: 虽然它们有些调皮，但其实做事很有逻辑，还很重情感。
t_en: Although they can be mischievous sometimes, they are very logical and they value relationships a lot.

t_ch: 比空间站的有些大人，要好多了……
t_en: They are so much better than the grown-ups in the space station...

t_ch: 为什么会选择研究珍稀物种
t_en: What made you study rare species?

t_ch: 珍稀物种的表达方式与思考逻辑，与成年人类是不相同的。
t_en: Their ways of expression and thinking are very different from human grown-ups.

t_ch: 但是不可否认，它们的处事方式与行为动机，有时候却往往比人类更有逻辑。
t_en: But their ways of handling things and the motives behind their behaviors are undeniably often more logical than that of the humans'.

t_ch: 大概就是这种逻辑感，让我更愿意研究它们吧。
t_en: It's probably their sense of logic that makes me want to study them.

t_ch: 你能听懂珍稀物种说话吗
t_en: Can you understand their language?

t_ch: 那是当然。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 别看我是个小孩子，我可是能听懂许多珍稀物种的语言的！
t_en: You might think I am a kid, but I understand many languages spoken by rare species!

t_ch: 它们的语言习惯和我们不是同一个维度，经常一个音节就能表示很多意思。
t_en: Their languages are not in the same dimension as ours. They use one syllable to express many different meanings.

t_ch: 比如呜呜伯的短呜和长呜，就是截然不同的含义。
t_en: For example, Wubbaboo's short and long whines mean completely different things.

t_ch: 如果你有兴趣，可以加入界种科，进行深度学习。
t_en: You can join the Department of Ecology to study it if you are interested.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I better leave you alone.

t_ch: 如果以后有珍稀物种相关的事件，可以告诉我哦。
t_en: Please keep me posted if you find out anything about rare species.

t_ch: 辛克尔_日常
t_en: Hinkel_Daily

t_ch: 有什么想要了解的，请尽快说明。灾后复兴工作很繁重。
t_en: Let me know if you have any questions. I'm very busy with the post-disaster recovery work.

t_ch: 我还有一些问题想要咨询…
t_en: I have a few more questions for you...

t_ch: 我有什么可以帮你的吗？
t_en: Is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 很遗憾，没有。
t_en: I'm afraid not.

t_ch: 你作为星穹列车的宾客，应该待在更加安全的区域。
t_en: As a guest from the Astral Express, you should stay in a safer area.

t_ch: 这份工作非常危险，如果你出了什么问题，我可没有办法给艾丝妲交代。
t_en: This job is very dangerous. If anything happens to you, I really don't know how I would explain it to Asta.

t_ch: 安全区的开辟工作很危险吧？
t_en: It must be dangerous to establish safe zones, right?

t_ch: 军团的余孽凭借那里错综复杂的路径，隐匿起身。
t_en: Remnants of the Legion are taking advantage of the complex terrain there to hide in.

t_ch: 稍不留意，就有可能被它们偷袭伏击。
t_en: You might get ambushed if you are not careful.

t_ch: 不过我们会在安全的基础上进行开辟，不会急于求成，这点你可以放心。
t_en: However, we will proceed with safety as our top priority. We are not in a rush. Don't worry.

t_ch: 灾后复兴工作，主要是围绕哪几个方面？
t_en: What are the main aspects of the work regarding post-disaster recovery?

t_ch: 除开基础建设方面的维护，最主要的方面还是科员的心理健康。
t_en: Apart from infrastructure maintenance, the most important aspect is the staff's mental health.

t_ch: 纵然灾难会毁灭物质，但是那些都有机会复原。唯有人心的损伤，是难以弥补的。
t_en: Disasters are physically destructive, but the buildings can be rebuilt. It is the human heart that is irreparable.

t_ch: 我还是不打扰你工作了。
t_en: I shouldn't keep you.

t_ch: 感谢你的理解，希望你远离还未开辟的受灾区域，注意自身安全。
t_en: Thank you for your understanding. Please stay away from the affected areas that have not opened up yet. Stay safe.

t_ch: 那些事情我会处理的，您不必担心。
t_en: I'll take care of those. Don't worry.

t_ch: 辛克尔那边的消息我刚才回复了，应该得到了审批就可以继续下一步流程。
t_en: I just replied to Hinkel. We should be able to move forward once it's approved.

t_ch: 稍等……请问这位科员，你是找我有什么事情吗？
t_en: Hold on... are you looking for me?

t_ch: 好久不见，有什么事情我可以帮您？
t_en: It's been a long time. How may I help you?

t_ch: 我还有一些事情想要问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 法政科会采取什么行动？
t_en: What action will the Department of Galactic Geopolitics take?

t_ch: 我只是法政科的一位向导而已，具体的方针策略，还得等创始人古恩老师决定。
t_en: I'm just a guide. Specific policies and strategies will be decided by the founder, Mr. Gunn.

t_ch: 但我相信，政法科一定会竭尽自己的努力，去弥补这场灾难导致的诸多漏洞。
t_en: But I believe the Department of Galactic Geopolitics will certainly try its best to make up for this disaster.

t_ch: 如果您感觉心理难受，我可以帮助您缓解一些压力。这也是我能为空间站做出的微薄贡献。
t_en: If you are not well mentally, I can help you relieve some stress. This is the least I can do for the space station.

t_ch: 你平日里也会给大家做心理疏导么？
t_en: Do you usually do psychological counseling for everyone?

t_ch: 在日常生活中，我更喜欢做一些思想实验。你可以理解为一些短问题测试。
t_en: Normally I prefer to do thought experiments, such as short quiz questions.

t_ch: 这些思想实验可以锻炼大家的思维，同时也能让我更加了解大家，与科员们相处。
t_en: Thought experiments can exercise people's mental capacity and help me understand them. It helps me to get along with the researchers.

t_ch: 等之后危机解除，你不妨也试一试？
t_en: When the crisis passes, would you like to have a try?

t_ch: 关于那位古恩老师…
t_en: About Mr. Gunn...

t_ch: 古恩老师是法政科的创始人，他富有智慧与哲思。
t_en: Mr. Gunn is the founder of the Department of Galactic Geopolitics. He is very wise and philosophical.

t_ch: 你可以与他多多交流沟通，想必会收获很多有趣的知识。
t_en: I am sure you will gain a lot of interesting knowledge if you talk to him more.

t_ch: 尤其是愚人戏方面，很难找到比他更懂的行家了。
t_en: Especially about Fool's Play. I don't think anyone will know more about it than him.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 能够解答您心中的困扰，我很荣幸。之后有需要，可以随时来与我联系。
t_en: It's my honor to answer your questions. Please contact me anytime if you have any further inquiries.

t_ch: 希拉脸上保持着永恒不变的笑容，你试图和这位美丽的向导交流。
t_en: Sheila's smile seems never-changing. You try to talk to this beautiful guide.

t_ch: 但任凭你如何询问，希拉的回应只有「<unbreak>159</unbreak>号脑部系统正在升级，给您带来的不便还请见谅！」。
t_en: But no matter what questions you ask, Sheila's reply is always the same, "No. <unbreak>159</unbreak> is currently going through a brain update. We apologize for any inconvenience caused."

t_ch: 好久不见，年轻人。这次有什么事儿吗？
t_en: Long time no see, kiddo. How can I help you?

t_ch: 我有一些问题…
t_en: I have some questions...

t_ch: 你来这里多久了？
t_en: How long have you been here?

t_ch: 具体的时间？记不清啦。
t_en: I don't remember exactly how long.

t_ch: 如你所见，我已经是一副老骨头了。
t_en: As you can see, I've got one foot in the grave.

t_ch: 在这里的时间转瞬即逝，对我而言，每一天都好像刚刚来到这里一样。
t_en: Time flies here. But for me, every day still feels like the first.

t_ch: 你是什么科室的研究员。
t_en: Which department are you from?

t_ch: 我和那位希拉女士是同一个科室的科员——法政科。
t_en: I came from the same department as Ms. Sheila, the Department of Galactic Geopolitics.

t_ch: 人老了，对于正义的探索已经比如年轻时那样热烈了。
t_en: I am old. My passion for justice is slowly fading away.

t_ch: 不过如果有年轻人想要与我交流一二，我还是很乐意聊聊的。
t_en: But if any young people fancy a discussion with me, I am more than happy to do that.

t_ch: 这场灾难，对你有影响吗？
t_en: Does this disaster affect you?

t_ch: 孩子，这是一场灾难，如你所言。
t_en: Kid, as you said, this is a disaster.

t_ch: 来到空间站的人，本就有一些是失去了家园的流离之辈。空间站对于他们而言，就是第二个家园。
t_en: Some people came to the space station because they lost their homes. The space station is like their second home.

t_ch: 不过幸好，空间站并没有失守，大家还能在这里，做实验，聊天，散步。
t_en: Fortunately, the space station survived. We can still do experiments, chat and stroll around.

t_ch: 真好，真好啊。
t_en: Good. It's really good.

t_ch: 我没有问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 好的孩子，以后有空，可以多多来聊聊。
t_en: Good. Kid, come and chat with me if you have time.

t_ch: 如果你对愚人戏有兴趣的话，就最好不过——我可知道不少有趣的戏呢！
t_en: If you are interested in Fool's Play, then you've come to the right person. I know many interesting ones!

t_ch: 又见到你了…除了星神…我了解的知识并不多……
t_en: We meet again... I don't know much about anything apart from the Aeons...

t_ch: 你有什么事情…吗？
t_en: How... can I help you?

t_ch: 我有些事情想问你…
t_en: I have a few questions for you...

t_ch: 是…哪个领域方面的知识？我不是通识类学者……
t_en: In what field of study? I am not a general scholar...

t_ch: 如果我回答不上来，还是请你去询问别的科员吧。
t_en: If I can't answer your questions, please ask the other researchers.

t_ch: 你说的那位大人是谁？
t_en: Who is the lord you were talking about?

t_ch: 哦！你也是对这次入侵事件感兴趣的科员吗？这件事可有来头了！
t_en: Oh! Are you also interested in the invasion? That was a huge event!

t_ch: 这次军团中的敌人，是反物质融铸而成的银河兵器——你知道这意味着什么吗？
t_en: The enemies in the Legion are cosmic weapons made of antimatter. Do you know what that means?

t_ch: 这正是对应了星神密卷里的痴缠与灾影啊！因此，我得出结论……
t_en: It echos the infatuation and disaster in the Mystic Scroll of Aeons! As a result, I've come to this conclusion...

t_ch: 这些入侵者，就是「毁灭」纳努克的追随者！绝不会有错！
t_en: These invaders are followers of Nanook the Destruction! This can't be wrong!

t_ch: 星神密卷是什么？
t_en: What is the Mystic Scroll of Aeons?

t_ch: 这就说来话长了。你可以理解为对于星神相关的文化记载。
t_en: It's a long story. You can say it's a record of cultures related to Aeons.

t_ch: 里面的遣词造句都非常晦涩难懂，我没日没夜地研读，也只能窥见其中一隅的哲思。
t_en: It is written in a very obscure fashion. Even though I've spent countless days and nights studying it, I can only understand a fraction of its philosophy.

t_ch: 不过有一点很重要，那就是星神密卷的内容，一定是根据现实撰写，而非是凭空捏造。
t_en: But one very important thing is that the content of the Mystic Scroll of Aeons is based on facts, not groundless fabrications.

t_ch: 这次的军团就是最好的证明！
t_en: This Legion is the best way to prove this!

t_ch: 你有见过那位大人吗？
t_en: Have you seen that lord?

t_ch: 见？！你在说些什么呢！
t_en: Have I seen that lord? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 古时候的人们不知晓有太阳，但却也知道崇拜太阳神。对他们而言，亲身体会太阳的灼热，是太过热烈的体验。
t_en: In ancient times, people were not aware of the exact nature of the sun but they still worshiped the Sun God. For them, the idea of approaching the scorching sun is too much for them to handle.

t_ch: 我能从现实中，考究到星神存在的蛛丝马迹，就已经很满足了。
t_en: I am already happy that I managed to find clues about the existence of Aeons.

t_ch: 如…如果还能被星神大人所注视的话……嘿嘿……
t_en: If... If I am really being watched by the great Aeons... Heheh...

t_ch: 我应该没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't think I have anything to ask.

t_ch: 如果你了解到其他与星神相关的线索，请记得与我联系！
t_en: Please contact me if you have any other clues about the Aeons!

t_ch: 最好是第一时间，只与我一个人联系！
t_en: Preferably right away, and only me!

t_ch: 小友，一日不见如隔三秋。
t_en: Buddy, it's been awhile.

t_ch: 小友若是还有任何想了解的事情，都可以问我。
t_en: Feel free to ask me if you have any questions.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 黑塔•万有应物科？
t_en: Herta Department of Implement Arts?

t_ch: 哈，应物科乃是黑塔的重要科室之一，包揽了站内所有资产的流入流出、盘查与清点。
t_en: Ha! An important department within the space station, Implement Arts is. We oversee the incoming and outgoing of all our assets, including inventory checks and counts.

t_ch: 并且，我们与「星际和平公司」保持着极为密切的交流。若你行走银河，耳目宽广，多少应该听过我们的名号。
t_en: Furthermore, we maintain close communications with Interastral Peace Corporation. For someone as well-traveled as yourself, I'm sure you've heard of our reputation.

t_ch: 奇物再收容小队？
t_en: Curio Collection Squad?

t_ch: 奇物再收容小队嘛，是空间站依据灾后复兴需要，临时牵头成立的应急小组。
t_en: The Curio Collection Squad is a temporary emergency unit established by the space station to address the needs of the post-disaster revival.

t_ch: 顾名思义，主要负责回收因怪物入侵而收容失效的奇物。
t_en: As the name suggests, it is mainly responsible for collecting the Curios that have been displaced due to the recent monster invasion.

t_ch: 安全区？
t_en: Safe zone?

t_ch: 就是你我二人脚下站定的位置，黑塔科员向「反物质军团」组织反击的据点。
t_en: It is the very ground on which we stand. The stronghold from which the space station researchers fought back against the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 虽说空间站已大体受控，但怪物还未被全数清除。
t_en: Although the situation is generally under control, the monsters have not been completely wiped out.

t_ch: 安全区就是过渡计划的产物，作为我们逐步夺回的舱段失地，最终将如星星之火，连为一体。
t_en: The safe zone is the result of our transition plan, for us to take back all the lost zones. I am confident all the dots will eventually be connected.

t_ch: 不用了，我自行领悟就好。
t_en: It's fine. I'll study it myself.

t_ch: 无妨，小友遇到任何困难，再来找明德便是。
t_en: Good. For any questions you may encounter in the future, my young friend, please do not hesitate to come to me.

t_ch: 日后，明德也还要多多仰仗小友。
t_en: In the future, there is much I must rely on you for.

t_ch: 我还有些事情需要问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 不然你还是和我一起去避难吧？
t_en: Why don't you take refuge with me?

t_ch: 不，我已经宣誓，用我的灵魂宣誓——必须踏过我的身体，才可入侵这里的生灵！
t_en: No, I took my vows. I swore on my soul — If anyone is to harm the creatures here, they'll have to step over my dead body.

t_ch: 在这场风暴结束之前，我绝不会，没错，卡波特绝不会前往避难！
t_en: Before this storm is over, I will never, ever, take refuge!

t_ch: 相信我，只要你前往主城区，你就会明白的。那里有许多如鲜花般绚烂的少女，正期许着我能够支撑起她们心灵的天空。
t_en: Trust me, you'll understand when you get to the town center. Many beautiful young ladies are expecting me to be the pillar of their souls.

t_ch: 一旦想到她们失望的眉眼，我的心比刀割还要刺痛。所以我不会，也绝不能前往避难。
t_en: It pains me whenever I think of the disappointment in their eyes. I will never take refuge.

t_ch: 你的每个动作和说话方式都好像精心设计过……
t_en: Your every movement and your ways of talking seem calculated...

t_ch: 我理解你的意思，之前也有很多人说过类似的话。
t_en: I understand what you mean. Many people have told me the same thing.

t_ch: 大家总之会被我的一些肢体语言或者是眼神所吸引注意，这一点我也十分困扰。
t_en: People always get attracted by my body language and my eyes. I find it really frustrating.

t_ch: 即便我努力克制，精心设计，希望不要为他人添麻烦，但也都是徒劳。
t_en: No matter how hard I try to refrain myself and avoid causing trouble for others, it never works.

t_ch: 或许这就是所说的，由内而外，无法控制吧？
t_en: Maybe it's just a part of me that's beyond my control.

t_ch: 你在这里真的不是在添乱吗？
t_en: Are you sure you are not causing trouble here?

t_ch: 希望你能够尊重我！我在这里的行为，不仅是为了我自己的灵魂誓言，更是为了众多生灵的平安！
t_en: Show me some respect, please! I do what I do, not only because I swore on my soul, but also for the sake of peace!

t_ch: 你可以不与我为伴，也可以不理解我的行动。但请你不要污蔑、践踏我的尊严！
t_en: I never asked you to join me or to understand my actions. But, please do not slander or trample on my dignity!

t_ch: 对你而言，我的语言或许是噪音；但对于真正的迷途之人而言，我的声音就是夜海的港灯！
t_en: My words may be noise to you, but for people who are lost, my voice is like a lighthouse in the stormy seas!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 无论如何，请你赶快前往避难。只有见到大家幸福的笑容，我才能安心。
t_en: Anyway, you should take refuge as soon as possible. I will be relieved when I see the smile on people's faces.

t_ch: 守护大家的心灵，坚守最后的港湾。这，就是我卡波特的职责。
t_en: Safeguard everyone's heart and defend our harbor. This is my job, Capote's job.

t_ch: 我还有些事情搞不懂…
t_en: There's something I can't wrap my head around...

t_ch: 那你可就找对人了，其实我正在调查这场袭击的内幕。
t_en: You've come to the right person. I am investigating this attack.

t_ch: 那你目前有什么发现？
t_en: What have you found so far?

t_ch: 凭我跟你现在的关系，我还不能把所有的消息都告诉你。
t_en: With our current relationship, I can't tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 但如果只是一些皮毛，那还可以说说。
t_en: But I can tell you something less important.

t_ch: 比如，这场袭击里有空间站的内鬼在搞事情。
t_en: For example, an insider from the space station was behind this attack.

t_ch: 虽然我手里还没拿到决定性的证据，但时间一定会证明我是对的。
t_en: Although I haven't found any conclusive evidence yet, time will prove me right.

t_ch: 袭击者到底有什么目的？
t_en: What was the attacker's purpose?

t_ch: 年轻人的问题问得很犀利啊。
t_en: Kiddo, your question is very sharp.

t_ch: 任何的架构都是不完善的，稍有不慎随时都会有坍塌的风险。
t_en: No structure is perfect. There is always a risk of collapse at any time.

t_ch: 造成这场袭击的原因是多方面的：防护不力，内外勾结……
t_en: There are many reasons behind this attack: Collusion, lack of defensive power...

t_ch: 但这些都不重要！重要的是，袭击发生后的现在空间站会发生什么样的改变！
t_en: But none of this matters! The only thing that matters is the changes brought to the space station by this attack!

t_ch: 接下来你打算怎么做？
t_en: What are you going to do?

t_ch: 富贵险中求，当然是挖地三尺把这个内鬼彻底揪出来。
t_en: To seek benefits, one must take risks. I am going to dig deep and find the rat.

t_ch: 放心，什么时候该冲，什么时候该逃，我心里有谱得很。
t_en: Don't worry about me, I know very well when I should act and when I should bolt.

t_ch: 倒是你，看着有些面生…啊，我知道了，你是星穹列车的那伙人！
t_en: And you, you don't look like you are from around here... Ah, I know. You're one of those people from the Astral Express!

t_ch: 外面来的家伙…我闻到了阴谋的味道。嘘，不要打扰我思考。
t_en: Outsiders... I smell a conspiracy. Shh, don't interrupt my train of thought.

t_ch: 我问完了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 你…唉，现在的年轻人，一点耐心都没有。
t_en: You... *sigh* Young people these days have no patience at all.

t_ch: 给你个忠告，出门在外要学会保护自己，知人知面不知心……
t_en: Here's my advice for you. Learn to protect yourself when you are outside. You never know what people are thinking.

t_ch: 朱红色的还是靛蓝色呢……难道我真的记错了？
t_en: Is it red or blue... did I make a mistake again?

t_ch: 那个…
t_en: Um...

t_ch: 嗯？您怎么欲言又止，不会是我记错了吧，应该不会啊…还是您也有什么事要交代我吗？
t_en: Yeah? Do you have something to say? Did I miss something again... Or do you have an order for me?

t_ch: 你刚才记的全错了。
t_en: You got it all wrong.

t_ch: 你别吓我啊？！这要是记错了，我的工作就会全面搁浅。
t_en: Don't scare me like that! If I get it wrong, I will lose my job.

t_ch: 不会吧……我再审查一遍好了…要是这次又出错，我肯定完蛋了……
t_en: I can't be... let me check again... If I make another mistake, I'll be dead...

t_ch: 需要帮忙吗？
t_en: Do you need help?

t_ch: 不用了不用了，卡波特大人强调过，他的私人事务不能随意经他人手。
t_en: No. Master Capote told me specifically that his personal affairs should not be handled by random strangers.

t_ch: 今天我的日程是，帮助卡波特大人「给粉丝回信」、「取回定制午餐」、「熨烫服装」、「给第二批粉丝回信」、「取回定制晚餐」、「给第三批粉丝回信」……
t_en: This is my schedule for the day: Help Master Capote reply to his fans, fetch his personalized lunch, iron clothes, reply to the second batch of his fans, fetch personalized dinner, reply to the third batch of his fans...

t_ch: 排得满满当当，喘口气的时间都快没了。这种时候，最忌讳忙中出错。
t_en: My schedule is so packed that I can't even spare time to breathe. I better not make any mistakes.

t_ch: 这些是你应该做的工作吗？
t_en: Are these supposed to be your job?

t_ch: 波少吩咐的工作，自然就是我应该做的工作啊。
t_en: Master Capote ordered me to do these things. Of course they are part of my job.

t_ch: 做秘书，最重要的就是完成方方面面的维护。工作的涵盖范围肯定就会广泛一些，边界也会相对模糊……
t_en: As his secretary, the most important thing is to keep track of everything. The scope of work can be large and the lines can be blurry sometimes...

t_ch: 虽然辛苦，但是多复习几次，肯定不会再出错了。
t_en: Although it's hard, I believe practice makes perfect.

t_ch: 我没什么要问了……
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 那我就继续了，如果我有打扰到你，请和我讲哦。
t_en: I should get back to work. Please let me know if I've interrupted you.

t_ch: 哦，又见面了。你没有遭遇不测，我也还尚且能够喘息，真是太难得了。
t_en: We meet again. You are still alive and I am still breathing. How rare is that!

t_ch: 还有一些事情想要问…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 你为什么不离开这里？
t_en: Why didn't you leave?

t_ch: 离开这里，又有什么地方是绝对安全的呢？稍微想一想就能知道答案。
t_en: Where can I find absolute safety if I leave? It's a no-brainer.

t_ch: 答案就是——无论身在何处，都没有绝对的安全！
t_en: The answer is, no matter where you go, no place is absolutely safe!

t_ch: 更何况，寰宇之间，应该很难有比这里更加安全的地方了吧？
t_en: Besides, I don't think there's any other place in the universe safer than here.

t_ch: 哎，说到底，待在这里，也是无奈啊……
t_en: *sigh* At the end of the day, I stayed because I don't really have a choice...

t_ch: 这次入侵对你造成了什么影响？
t_en: How did the invasion affect your life?

t_ch: 我其实很多天没有睡过一次好觉了。
t_en: In fact, I haven't had a good sleep for many days.

t_ch: 而且……我总是觉得，身边会突然出现一道裂缝。而那些怪物，就会从裂缝里缓缓走出来……
t_en: And... I always feel like a crack might suddenly appear next to me. And those monsters will slowly emerge out of the crack...

t_ch: 啊，这一切真的是太可怕了！我不知道要过多久，才能彻底遗忘！
t_en: Ahhh, this is really terrible! I don't know how long it will take before I can get this out of my head!

t_ch: 对于复兴建设，你有没有什么想法？
t_en: What's your take on the recovery construction?

t_ch: 我记得艾丝妲站长已经安排了相关部门在进行专门的复兴与安全区开辟吧？
t_en: I remember Lead Researcher Asta has organized the relevant departments to work on the recovery and establishment of safe zones, right?

t_ch: 不过……除了展望未来进行复兴，还有很多需要完成的事情吧？
t_en: However... Other than looking towards the future, there is still a lot to be done.

t_ch: 那些在这次灾难中失去生命的人……甚至连铭牌都没能被完整保存……
t_en: People who've lost their lives in this disaster... Some of them didn't even have an intact nameplate left behind...

t_ch: 那他们又该有谁去铭记，谁去哀悼呢？
t_en: Who will remember them? And who is going to mourn them?

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 哎，总之，还是要注意安全。现在的情况，发生什么意外都不足为奇……
t_en: In short, please be careful. The way things are right now, anything can happen.

t_ch: 哎，生命无常，生死难料。说不定这一次见面，就是我们最后一次呢？
t_en: *sigh* Life is unpredictable. Who knows if this will be the last time we meet?

t_ch: 好久不见，我的好朋友。你最近都在忙些什么？倘若有任何地方需要我的帮助，请直接开口吧！为知己两肋插刀，是我的人生座右铭。
t_en: It's been a while, my friend. What have you been doing? Let me know if you need my help! It's my life motto to help my friends the best I can!

t_ch: 我还有一些问题想要问…
t_en: I still got some questions for you...

t_ch: 尽管问吧，我的伙伴。我一定知无不言言无不尽，解答你心中所有的疑惑。
t_en: Any questions you have, my friend. I will tell you everything I know to solve all the questions in your heart.
